Improve any engine without redeployment:

• engine_config.json: RSS feeds, Google News templates, caps
• backfill_engine.py: VC portfolio URLs

A. WEEKLY SOURCING MACHINE — Full Calendar

Websets Search — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 10 leads

Websets engine not configured for this thesis. Add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine in Airtable to enable.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Enable Websets: in Airtable, add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine with enabled: true, count_requested: 25, target_per_run: 5, and 2 engines (ws_discovery, ws_funding) each with a 7-day query_rotation.
Exa Semantic Search — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 17 leads

Exa neural semantic search using category='company' → finds startup homepages matching your “Here is a…” query. No date filter: category='company' does not support freshness. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness (seen URLs/names are skipped).

Rotation: prod=2/day per thesis, test=1/day per thesis cycling through 14 total | Results: 5/query test, 25/query prod | Cost: $0.008 test / $0.046 prod
THIS RUN — 2 queries for Profund VC (test mode: 2 of 2 prod queries):
1. ai_workflow_automation“Here is a French AI workflow automation startup building LLM-powered tools for HR or finance teams in French SMEs and mid-market companies.”
2. ai_workflow_automation“Here is a pre-revenue European AI workflow co-pilot startup integrating with ERP or CRM to automate data entry and approval chains at pre-seed stage.”
ⓘ No date filter — Exa category='company' does not support freshness filtering. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's exa_queries. Write “Here is a company that [does X for Y customers]” describing a company you'd invest in. Best queries are specific about the customer type and problem (e.g. “Here is a B2B SaaS startup helping hotel chains automate their RFP process”). Runs 2/day prod (1/day test), rotating through all queries over N days.
Exa Similar (Lookalike) — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 20 leads

Exa find_similar() → sends competitor/MATCH homepage URLs and returns companies with similar website content based on neural embedding similarity. Does not support category='company' so some non-company pages may appear (filtered by gate). Competitor domains are automatically excluded from results.

Seeds: 30 competitor anchors + 0 MATCH URLs = 30 total | Rotation: prod=5/day, test=2/day | Cost: $0.016 test / $0.065 prod
THIS RUN — 2 seeds for Profund VC (test mode: 2 of 5 prod seeds):
1. Naology (anchor)
2. Zelros (anchor)
ⓘ No date filter — find_similar does not support freshness. Deduplication handles freshness. MATCHes become seeds in future runs.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add seeds: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's competitor_anchors. Add exact company names (e.g. “Harvey”, “Ironclad”). Each name is converted to https://www.{name}.com and used as a seed URL. Pick direct competitors in your target niche — broad platforms (SAP, Salesforce) produce too many irrelevant lookalikes. Every MATCH you approve in Airtable auto-becomes a seed in future runs.
Google News RSS — Profund VC
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 40 articles

Fetches fresh Google News RSS with after:yesterday filter — always the last 24h. Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names mentioned. Runs ALL queries every day (no rotation): freshness comes from the after:yesterday filter.

Rotation: NONE — all queries daily | Cost: $0.00 (free Google RSS) | Test cap: 40 articles, Prod: 150
Fetched: 40 articles → Gemini miner → 7 companies → evaluated: 2 for Profund VC

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → each angle's apify_linkedin_posts_queries. Each phrase targets a different signal type. Best patterns: hiring signal (“hiring VP Sales [sector]”), funding signal (“raised seed [sector]”), product launch (“just launched [product type]”). 1 query runs per day per thesis — the full pool rotates over N days.
LinkedIn Jobs — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Apify LinkedIn Jobs → job postings from the last 24h. scrapeCompany=true returns the company website URL directly. Filter f_CS=1,2 restricts to 1–50 employee companies. A startup actively hiring CFO, VP Sales, or engineers in your niche = strong growth signal. 1 entry/day per thesis with weekly angle rotation.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 14 total · 2 angles rotating weekly | f_TPR=r86400 (24h) | f_CS=1,2 (1–50 empl.) | sortBy=DD | Cost: ~$0.03–$0.08/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_linkedin_jobs_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry targets 1 day of the week: keywords (e.g. “CFO hotel technology”) + city (e.g. “Paris”). Best signals: C-level hires (CFO, CTO, VP Sales) = fundraising/growth in progress. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). f_CS=1,2 keeps companies 1–50 employees. f_TPR=r86400 = last 24h only.
Fundraising — EU Funding News (Profund VC)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 21 leads

Fundraising scraper (Apify). Scrapes latest EU funding news from TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, FinSMEs. ⚠ We cannot target a specific sector — the actor always fetches the latest news. Today-only filter applied post-fetch. All results then evaluated by the LLM (which is the real sector filter).

Shared engine (same results for all theses) | Rotation: daily — same empty payload every run | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.20/run | Test: 10 leads, Prod: 30 leads

Additionally, the company is based in the United States, failing the geographic criteria for both angles (French SMEs/regulated intermediaries).https://www.finsmes.com/2026/05/vori-raises-22m-in
Astranis0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (CORE business) fails. Nyobolt0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails. Nyobolt's CORE business is hardware/materials (fast-charging battery technology), not B2B software or AI workflow.https://www.finsmes.com/2026/05/nyobolt-raises-60m

District0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails because the company's core business is an AI-powered commerce platform for creators and brands, not an AI workflow, or regulated-intermediary technology for B2B. Pronto30NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) failed. Corgi0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. 🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
This engine scrapes TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, and FinSMEs automatically — no sector targeting is possible. The LLM evaluation step is the real filter. To improve precision, refine the thesis gate questions in your fund_thesis.
Crunchbase — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 3 companies scanned

Apify Crunchbase → 1 ultra-precise query/day: exact city + exact Crunchbase industry enum + employee band. operatingStatus=Active, companyType=For Profit. Returns 5–25 highly filtered companies. Weekly angle rotation cycles through all angles.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 14 total · 2 angles rotating weekly | Cost: ~$0.07–$0.42/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_crunchbase_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry: exact Crunchbase industry enum + city + employee band. Use ultra-specific enums (e.g. “Hotel”, “Legal Tech”, “Electronic Health Record (EHR)”) — broad enums like “Travel” or “Financial Technology” return thousands of irrelevant results. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). Country is auto-filled from the city — never add a separate country field.
RSS — EU Startup Media (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 50 leads

RSS feeds from EU startup media (Sifted, TechCrunch EU, Maddyness, etc.) → Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names. Runs ALL feeds every day (cap: 50 articles test, 500 prod). Shared across all theses.

Shared (evaluated per-thesis) | Rotation: NONE — all feeds daily (cap limits) | Test cap: 50 articles | Prod cap: 500 articles
⚠ Known: some sites obfuscate company names → garbled names filtered before LLM evaluation

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add feeds: open execution/config/engine_config.json, find rss_feeds, and append any RSS URL from EU startup/VC media. No Airtable change needed. Raise article_cap to fetch more per run (test=50, prod=500). To improve precision, add media sources focused on your specific sector (e.g. healthtech newsletters, legaltech blogs).
Backfill — VC Portfolios & Directories (Shared)
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 152 leads

Firecrawl + Gemini 2.5 Flash → scrapes VC/accelerator portfolio pages and extracts portfolio companies. Rotates through all sources (~9/day). Gemini reads the raw HTML and returns a JSON list of {name, url, description}.

Shared | Rotation: 9 sources/day (68 ÷ 7) | Test: 2 sources, Prod: 9 sources | Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add a VC firm: find their portfolio page URL, add it to BACKFILL_SOURCES in execution/sourcing_v3/engines/backfill_engine.py. Format: "Firm Name": "https://firm.com/portfolio". It auto-rotates into the weekly cycle (total_sources ÷ 7 per day). Test mode scrapes only 2 sources/run to limit Firecrawl costs. Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash per page.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add codes: append NAF codes to naf_codes in Airtable shared config (under sourcing_config.shared). Find relevant codes at pappers.fr/code-naf. To change the lookback window, edit pappers_config.lookback_days in engine_config.json (default: 90 days). Current codes are listed above.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.

There is no evidence to evaluate STEP 1 or any active angle.https://github.com/Autoloops/upskill
Show HN: Recursant – service mesh f20NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. The company develops a "service mesh for governing AI agents", which is an infrastructure layer for AI. This does not fit the specific B2B SaaS criteria for AI workflow automation targeting operations, finance, legal, or HR teams, nor does it fit the regulated intermediary fintech criteria. It's a foundational AI technology rather than an application layer for specific business functions.https://www.recursant.ai/
Show HN: Recursant – service mesh f10NO MATCHINSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE — not enough info on company activity to evaluate STEP 1. 🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.
CRT.sh — Certificate Transparency (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Monitors Certificate Transparency logs (crt.sh) for new domain registrations matching sector keywords. A new domain with “legalai”, “hoteltech” etc. in the name = early signal of a new startup. Free, runs daily (test: 3 keywords, prod: 10 keywords).

Shared | Rotation: NONE — daily | Cost: $0.00 (free crt.sh) | Prod cap: 50 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sector keywords: edit cert_transparency_keywords in engine_config.json. Use compound words founders typically include in domain names (e.g. “legalai”, “hotelops”, “medtech”, “travelops”). Avoid generic terms like “software” or “tech” — too many false positives. Runs automatically in prod every day.

Summary: What they do - EMYE offers a single, AI-native HR Software as a Service (SaaS) platform that unifies payroll, leave/vacation management, recruitment, training, and talent management into one easy-to-use system. - The platform emphasizes automation and reducing manual wo…

The company EmyeHR offers an AI-native HR SaaS platform unifying payroll, recruitment, and talent management, specifically targeting French SMEs and mid-market companies, which aligns perfectly with the 'ai_workflow_automation' angle for HR teams in France (STEP 1 & STEP 2).

https://primmo.co
Primmo is an AI assistant designed for notaries and law firms. Its features focus on saving time across daily tasks by integrating with common tools and data sources: - Email automation: integrates with Outlook to pre-draft emails, rename/compress/categorize attachments, detect …

sforbt
3 MATCH · 100 NO MATCH · 3% hit rate
🌿 Evergreen Multi-Engine Sourcing System
Improve any engine without redeployment:
• Airtable: exa_queries, competitor_anchors, sectors, naf_codes
• engine_config.json: RSS feeds, Google News templates, caps
• backfill_engine.py: VC portfolio URLs
• New MATCHes auto-seeded into Exa Similar for next cycle.

A. WEEKLY SOURCING MACHINE — Full Calendar

Websets Search — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 10 leads

Websets engine not configured for this thesis. Add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine in Airtable to enable.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Enable Websets: in Airtable, add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine with enabled: true, count_requested: 25, target_per_run: 5, and 2 engines (ws_discovery, ws_funding) each with a 7-day query_rotation.
Exa Semantic Search — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 17 leads

Exa neural semantic search using category='company' → finds startup homepages matching your “Here is a…” query. No date filter: category='company' does not support freshness. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness (seen URLs/names are skipped).

Rotation: prod=2/day per thesis, test=1/day per thesis cycling through 28 total | Results: 5/query test, 25/query prod | Cost: $0.008 test / $0.046 prod
THIS RUN — 2 queries for sforbt (test mode: 2 of 2 prod queries):
1. hotel_booking_saas“Here is a French or German startup providing a corporate hotel marketplace with negotiated rates and automated booking for mid-market firms with significant travel budgets.”
2. hotel_booking_saas“Here is a Here is an early-stage European SaaS company at Seed or Series A building hotel sourcing and RFP management for travel procurement teams in professional services firms.”
ⓘ No date filter — Exa category='company' does not support freshness filtering. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness.
QUERY POOL — Full Rotation Cycle (28-day full cycle · test: 1/day per thesis · 28 total):
hotel_booking_saas weight=5 — 7 entries
in 13d — Wed May 20“Here is a European B2B SaaS platform automating corporate hotel booking and preferred hotel program management for enterprise travel managers, replacing manual GDS searches.”
in 14d — Thu May 21“Here is a hotel booking technology sold to Procurement Officers and CFOs in European multinationals seeking centralized lodging spend visibility and policy compliance.”
in 15d — Fri May 22“Here is a French or German startup providing a corporate hotel marketplace with negotiated rates and automated booking for mid-market firms with significant travel budgets.”

EmyeHR - Suite IA tout-en-un Paie e10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (core business) fails because EmyeHR is an all-in-one HR platform focusing on payroll, recruitment, and talent management. While HR can touch upon employee travel, its core business is not corporate travel technology.https://emye.fr
Phacet0NO MATCHPhacet provides 'financial control automation' using AI for finance teams to prepare, reconcile, validate, and close financial data across various industries. This is a generic financial automation tool and does not specialize in, nor does it explicitly mention, any aspect of corporate travel technology (hotel booking, corporate payments for travel, TMC workflow, or ERP integration specific to travel spend). It fails STEP A of the gate question as its core business is not within the European corporate travel technology domain.https://phacetlabs.com
Flowie10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. Flowie's core business is general finance and procurement process orchestration, Intake-to-Pay, and electronic invoicing, not specifically within the European corporate travel technology domain. 🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's exa_queries. Write “Here is a company that [does X for Y customers]” describing a company you'd invest in. Best queries are specific about the customer type and problem (e.g. “Here is a B2B SaaS startup helping hotel chains automate their RFP process”). Runs 2/day prod (1/day test), rotating through all queries over N days.
Exa Similar (Lookalike) — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 20 leads

Exa find_similar() → sends competitor/MATCH homepage URLs and returns companies with similar website content based on neural embedding similarity. Does not support category='company' so some non-company pages may appear (filtered by gate). Competitor domains are automatically excluded from results.

Seeds: 60 competitor anchors + 0 MATCH URLs = 60 total | Rotation: prod=5/day, test=2/day | Cost: $0.016 test / $0.065 prod
THIS RUN — 2 seeds for sforbt (test mode: 2 of 5 prod seeds):
1. NexTravel (anchor)
2. Travelport (anchor)
ⓘ No date filter — find_similar does not support freshness. Deduplication handles freshness. MATCHes become seeds in future runs.

Okaxis is a generic customer insights platform, which is neither within the corporate travel technology domain nor specifically focused on hotel booking, TMC workflow, corporate travel payments, or travel ERP integrations.https://okaxis.com
OpenINSIGHTS0NO MATCHOpenINSIGHTS is an IT services and consulting firm focused on retail-focused predictive analytics and customer insights, which does not align with any of the European corporate travel technology angles in STEP A or B.https://openinsights.ci
Crosswalk0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. Crosswalk is a data infrastructure and analytics company providing a zero-party data platform, primarily for financial institutions. This is not within the European corporate travel technology domain.https://crosswalknyc.com
Behavio0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails. Behavio is a market research company specializing in behavioral market research tools, ad testing, and brand tracking, which is not within the European corporate travel technology domain.https://behaviolabs.com
ThroughPut.ai0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. ThroughPut.ai provides supply chain intelligence and analytics, which is not related to European corporate travel technology.https://throughput.world
Zencargo0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. Zencargo is a global freight logistics provider and supply chain management platform, which is not within the European corporate travel technology domain.https://zencargo.com
ketteQ10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. ketteQ specializes in AI-driven supply chain planning software, not corporate travel technology.https://ketteq.com
Optimise Logistics10NO MATCHSTEP 1 FAIL. Optimise Logistics provides a digital platform for freight shipping, connecting shippers with carriers, which is logistics for goods, not corporate travel technology.https://optimiseco.com
numi solutions0NO MATCHThe company provides an AI-powered supply chain platform and does not operate in the European corporate travel technology domain. This fails STEP A of the gate question.https://numi.digital
Engine40NO MATCHThe company operates a modern travel platform for booking and managing work trips globally, including Europe, which fits the general domain (A). However, its primary focus as described is a direct booking and management platform for corporations and their employees, which does not directly align with any of the specified 'active angles' like a hotel booking marketplace sold to Travel Managers, a workflow platform for Travel Management Companies (TMCs), corporate travel payments with specific features, or ERP integration middleware. While it touches on aspects like centralized spend visibility and single-invoice billing, these are features of a broader corporate booking tool rather than the specialized angles defined in the MATCH CRITERIA.https://engine.com
BizTrip AI20NO MATCHSTEP 1 (European focus) fails. BizTrip AI is explicitly headquartered in San Francisco, U.S., with no mention of European operations or target market.https://biztrip.ai
Oversee30NO MATCHThe company provides B2B software for travel technology, aligning with the general domain. However, the DESCRIPTION does not specify a focus on the European market, which is a core requirement for a MATCH. Therefore it fails STEP A. The company also does not specifically align with any of the required sub-angles (hotel booking SaaS, TMC workflow, corporate travel payments, or travel ERP integrations for Europe).https://oversee.biz
TWAI20NO MATCHThe company provides general travel technology solutions for suppliers (airlines, airports), agencies, and distribution. While it operates in the travel tech space, its core activities described do not specifically align with business travel hotel booking, TMC workflow, corporate travel payments, or ERP integrations for European companies. It appears to be a broader travel distribution and API provider, failing to meet the specific criteria of STEP A related to corporate travel technology within Europe.https://twai.com
ATS – Advanced Travel Software25NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed. The company offers an Online Reservation System for general travel agencies and tour operators, supporting air tickets, hotels, train tickets, and car rentals. This is a generic travel booking platform for agents and operators, not specifically focused on European corporate travel management, hotel sourcing, TMC workflow, corporate payments, or ERP integrations for business travel.https://ats-ors.com
Deem, Inc.30NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails due to lack of explicit European focus. While Deem has a global presence with offices in Dublin, Ireland, and operations in 12 countries, the core description does not specify its primary market or revenue focus within Europe, which is a key criterion for this MA thesis.https://deem.com

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add seeds: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's competitor_anchors. Add exact company names (e.g. “Harvey”, “Ironclad”). Each name is converted to https://www.{name}.com and used as a seed URL. Pick direct competitors in your target niche — broad platforms (SAP, Salesforce) produce too many irrelevant lookalikes. Every MATCH you approve in Airtable auto-becomes a seed in future runs.
Google News RSS — sforbt
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 40 articles

Fetches fresh Google News RSS with after:yesterday filter — always the last 24h. Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names mentioned. Runs ALL queries every day (no rotation): freshness comes from the after:yesterday filter.

Rotation: NONE — all queries daily | Cost: $0.00 (free Google RSS) | Test cap: 40 articles, Prod: 150
Fetched: 40 articles → Gemini miner → 7 companies → evaluated: 2 for sforbt

LinkedIn Posts — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ 0 posts this run

Apify LinkedIn Post Search → scans posts from the last 7 days (postedLimit=week, sortBy=date). Gemini Flash content miner reads each post and extracts company names from the text. 1 query/day per thesis, rotating through all queries.

Per-thesis | Rotation: test=2/day (1/thesis), prod=2/day (1/thesis) cycling through 16 queries | postedLimit: week | sortBy: date | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.10/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → each angle's apify_linkedin_posts_queries. Each phrase targets a different signal type. Best patterns: hiring signal (“hiring VP Sales [sector]”), funding signal (“raised seed [sector]”), product launch (“just launched [product type]”). 1 query runs per day per thesis — the full pool rotates over N days.
LinkedIn Jobs — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Apify LinkedIn Jobs → job postings from the last 24h. scrapeCompany=true returns the company website URL directly. Filter f_CS=1,2 restricts to 1–50 employee companies. A startup actively hiring CFO, VP Sales, or engineers in your niche = strong growth signal. 1 entry/day per thesis with weekly angle rotation.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | f_TPR=r86400 (24h) | f_CS=1,2 (1–50 empl.) | sortBy=DD | Cost: ~$0.03–$0.08/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_linkedin_jobs_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry targets 1 day of the week: keywords (e.g. “CFO hotel technology”) + city (e.g. “Paris”). Best signals: C-level hires (CFO, CTO, VP Sales) = fundraising/growth in progress. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). f_CS=1,2 keeps companies 1–50 employees. f_TPR=r86400 = last 24h only.
Fundraising — EU Funding News (sforbt)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 21 leads

Fundraising scraper (Apify). Scrapes latest EU funding news from TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, FinSMEs. ⚠ We cannot target a specific sector — the actor always fetches the latest news. Today-only filter applied post-fetch. All results then evaluated by the LLM (which is the real sector filter).

Shared engine (same results for all theses) | Rotation: daily — same empty payload every run | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.20/run | Test: 10 leads, Prod: 30 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
This engine scrapes TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, and FinSMEs automatically — no sector targeting is possible. The LLM evaluation step is the real filter. To improve precision, refine the thesis gate questions in your fund_thesis.
Crunchbase — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 3 companies scanned

Apify Crunchbase → 1 ultra-precise query/day: exact city + exact Crunchbase industry enum + employee band. operatingStatus=Active, companyType=For Profit. Returns 5–25 highly filtered companies. Weekly angle rotation cycles through all angles.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | Cost: ~$0.07–$0.42/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_crunchbase_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry: exact Crunchbase industry enum + city + employee band. Use ultra-specific enums (e.g. “Hotel”, “Legal Tech”, “Electronic Health Record (EHR)”) — broad enums like “Travel” or “Financial Technology” return thousands of irrelevant results. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). Country is auto-filled from the city — never add a separate country field.
RSS — EU Startup Media (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 50 leads

RSS feeds from EU startup media (Sifted, TechCrunch EU, Maddyness, etc.) → Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names. Runs ALL feeds every day (cap: 50 articles test, 500 prod). Shared across all theses.

Shared (evaluated per-thesis) | Rotation: NONE — all feeds daily (cap limits) | Test cap: 50 articles | Prod cap: 500 articles
⚠ Known: some sites obfuscate company names → garbled names filtered before LLM evaluation

Steller10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. Steller is an app for sharing travel stories, which is a consumer leisure-focused product, not B2B software or tech-enabled services for corporate travel.https://steller.co/skift.com/feed
Spirit Airlines0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. Spirit Airlines is a direct-to-consumer airline, not a B2B corporate travel technology company operating in Europe. It does not provide hotel booking, travel policy enforcement, corporate payment reconciliation, TMC workflow, or ERP integration for business travel.https://www.spirit.com/s/about-usskift.com/feed
Lola10NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed. The company provides financial consulting services, not B2B software, SaaS, or tech-enabled services for corporate travel.https://www.lola-consulting.com/skift.com/feed
Amadeus90MATCHAmadeus is a foundational provider in the travel technology space, offering extensive solutions across airport operations, passenger services, and ground handling, which includes B2B software for airlines and travel agencies. Their focus on 'Amadeus for Startups' and the descriptions of their services align with providing B2B SaaS solutions within the European corporate travel domain, particularly supporting TMC workflow and ERP integrations implicitly.https://amadeus.com/en/startupsskift.com/feed
SNCF10NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed. SNCF is a French national railway company, not a B2B software, SaaS, or tech-enabled services provider for corporate travel technology. While they innovate, their core business is transportation, and their digital arm focuses on improving internal operations and consumer services related to train travel, not B2B travel tech.https://numerique.sncf.com/startup/skift.com/feed
Expedia30NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. Expedia is a global online travel agency (OTA) with a strong consumer focus, as well as B2B offerings. While it has B2B components, its core business is not exclusively European corporate travel technology, nor does it primarily sell SaaS to Travel Managers, TMCs, or FDs for corporate travel problems based on the DESCRIPTION. The company's description emphasizes connecting partners to 'the universe of travelers,' which aligns more with a broad OTA model rather than targeted B2B European corporate travel tech. This means it doesn't align with the specific B2B SaaS criteria for any of the active angles.https://expediagroup.com/skift.com/feed
Booking.com10NO MATCHBooking.com's core business is explicitly stated as a digital travel company focused on making it easier for 'everyone' to experience the world, connecting 'millions of travelers' and enabling 'properties around the world to reach a global audience'. This is a clear consumer-facing platform for leisure travel, failing Step A of the gate question which requires a focus on B2B corporate travel technology.https://www.booking.com/content/about.htmlskift.com/feed
Five20NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. The company, Five, described as helping to 'Launch your startup' and providing 'Startup Advisors backed by a community of founders', is a generic startup incubation/support platform, not a business travel technology company operating in Europe.https://www.startups.com/sifted.eu/feed
Zopa10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails. Zopa is a digital bank offering general financial products, not specifically focused on European corporate travel technology or any aspect of hotel booking, travel policy, payment reconciliation, or TMC workflow.https://f4.fund/startups/zopasifted.eu/feed
CodeWords0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. The company, Codeword, is a communication agency, not a business travel technology company.https://www.codewordagency.com/sifted.eu/feed
Lovable0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) explicitly requires the company's core business to be within the European corporate travel technology domain. 🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add feeds: open execution/config/engine_config.json, find rss_feeds, and append any RSS URL from EU startup/VC media. No Airtable change needed. Raise article_cap to fetch more per run (test=50, prod=500). To improve precision, add media sources focused on your specific sector (e.g. healthtech newsletters, legaltech blogs).

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add a VC firm: find their portfolio page URL, add it to BACKFILL_SOURCES in execution/sourcing_v3/engines/backfill_engine.py. Format: "Firm Name": "https://firm.com/portfolio". It auto-rotates into the weekly cycle (total_sources ÷ 7 per day). Test mode scrapes only 2 sources/run to limit Firecrawl costs. Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash per page.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add codes: append NAF codes to naf_codes in Airtable shared config (under sourcing_config.shared). Find relevant codes at pappers.fr/code-naf. To change the lookback window, edit pappers_config.lookback_days in engine_config.json (default: 90 days). Current codes are listed above.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.
CRT.sh — Certificate Transparency (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Monitors Certificate Transparency logs (crt.sh) for new domain registrations matching sector keywords. A new domain with “legalai”, “hoteltech” etc. in the name = early signal of a new startup. Free, runs daily (test: 3 keywords, prod: 10 keywords).

Shared | Rotation: NONE — daily | Cost: $0.00 (free crt.sh) | Prod cap: 50 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sector keywords: edit cert_transparency_keywords in engine_config.json. Use compound words founders typically include in domain names (e.g. “legalai”, “hotelops”, “medtech”, “travelops”). Avoid generic terms like “software” or “tech” — too many false positives. Runs automatically in prod every day.
